examples: Included among these cells are hairs (leaves have hairs), any sort of bump or ornamentation, and most important, stomata.
Epidermal cells on areas with stomata are polygonous.
Plants breath in air and breath out moisture through tiny pores called stomata.
The stomata stay closed for longer, and less water is lost to the atmosphere.
Driven by the need to conserve water, plants produce fewer stomata openings in their leaves when there is more carbon dioxide in the air.
